# terrible_ravendb_ravendb_database (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `database_settings` (String) Dictionary of database-level settings to apply. Keys and values are normalized to strings and compared against current customized settings. When differences exist, the module updates settings and toggles the database state to reload them safely.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `encrypted` (Boolean) Create the database as encrypted. When `true`, the module ensures a secret key is assigned (generated or read from file) and distributed to all cluster nodes before creation. Requires `certificate_path` to access admin endpoints.
- `encryption_key` (String) Path to a file that contains the raw encryption key (plain text). Mutually exclusive with `generate_encryption_key`. Used only when `encrypted=true`.
- `encryption_key_output_path` (String) When `generate_encryption_key=true`, write the generated key to this local file with safe permissions. Ignored if `generate_encryption_key=false`.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `generate_encryption_key` (Boolean) If `true`, asks the server to generate a new encryption key via the admin API. Mutually exclusive with `encryption_key`. Used only when `encrypted=true`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `replication_factor` (Number) Number of server nodes to replicate the database to. Must be a positive integer. Only used when creating a database. Required on creation; ignored for existing databases.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Desired state of the database. If `present`, the database will be created if it does not exist, and settings will be reconciled. If `absent`, the database will be deleted if it exists. If omitted (`null`), the module reconciles settings on an existing database but will not create or delete it. If the database does not exist and `state` is omitted, the task fails with guidance to use `state=present`.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `topology_members` (String) Optional list of cluster node tags to host this database (fixed placement). When provided, its length must equal `replication_factor`. Honored only on creation. If the database already exists, providing `topology_members` will fail.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `msg` (String) Human-readable message describing the result or error.
